pregame_model_read.pre_bet_thesis: Bibee 6+ at +134 was a secondary plus-money position. The model preferred 6+ over the juiced 5+ market because it offered cleaner price shape, but tagged the play with sinker-overuse/contact-volume risk after prior misses.

pitch_notes.sinker: 30 pitches, 0 K, 0 whiffs on 11 swings, 30% CSW. Heavy usage again suppressed K ceiling even while run prevention remained excellent.
pitch_notes.cutter: 27 pitches, 1 K, 33% whiffs on swings, 33% CSW. Best functional putaway pitch but not enough by itself.
pitch_notes.changeup: 11 pitches, 1 K, 33% whiffs on swings, 18% CSW. Low-volume secondary piece.
pitch_notes.curveball: 9 pitches, 0 K, 0 whiffs on 5 swings, 22% CSW. Did not create chase or whiff value.
pitch_notes.sweeper: 5 pitches, 1 K, 33% whiffs on swings, 20% CSW. Low-volume flash only.
pitch_notes.four_seam_fastball: 5 pitches, 0 K, 0 whiffs on 2 swings, 20% CSW. Minimal role.

what_failed: Bibee delivered an elite real-life start but only 3 strikeouts across 8.0 innings.
The model correctly flagged sinker-overuse/contact-volume risk, but still allowed the secondary 6+ position.
Whiff rate reached only 17% on swings, below the required level for a 6+ strikeout ticket.
Chase rate was only 22%, limiting expansion and putaway opportunities.
Sinker was the highest-usage pitch at 34% and produced zero whiffs, reinforcing the contact-management profile.
Bibee's contact suppression and efficiency created innings, but the same efficiency came through balls in play rather than strikeout accumulation.
what_was_correct: The model avoided the expensive 5+ at -161 and limited exposure to a small plus-money secondary stake.
The pregame patch tag correctly identified sinker-heavy contact risk as the main danger.
Leash/runway was not the problem; he threw 87 pitches and completed 8 innings.
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: Upgrade Bibee's sinker-heavy profile to a hard filter: do not play 6+ unless recent cutter/curve/sweeper whiff indicators are elevated or the opponent has a clear swing-and-miss profile. Treat Bibee more as a run-prevention/inning-efficiency arm than an automatic K-over arm when sinker usage projects above 28-30%.

notes: This loss is a classic full-runway K miss. Bibee was excellent as a pitcher but not as a strikeout prop. The key lesson is that efficiency and innings do not equal K runway when the pitch mix leans sinker/contact management.
